title: Ready to Upgrade Your Workout? Start With These Prime Day Deals
gist: Wired's Prime Day fitness tech roundup covers smartwatches, walking pads, and recovery gear, with the Apple Watch Series 11 reaching parity pricing with the SE 3—both at $279—temporarily collapsing Apple's own entry-level/premium price ladder in wearables.

insights.claim: Prime Day discounting brought the Apple Watch Series 11 to $279—the same price as the discounted Apple Watch SE 3—eliminating the usual $50–$120 premium gap and making the entry model an irrational purchase at current sale pricing.
insights.speaker: Boutayna Chokrane
insights.so_what: Temporary ASP compression at the Apple Watch tier signals aggressive retail partner discounting; investors should watch whether Apple allows this to persist post-Prime Day or tightens MAP enforcement.
insights.quote: Right now, the Series 11 is priced the same as the better value SE 3 (44 mm)—which is also on sale. Don't miss out on this steal.
insights.locator: Best Smartwatch for iPhone Owners section

insights.claim: Google Pixel Watch 4 is the first repairable smartwatch from Google, allowing battery and display replacement via iFixit parts—a durability differentiator in a category otherwise dominated by planned obsolescence.
insights.speaker: Boutayna Chokrane
insights.so_what: Right-to-repair as a product feature signals Google responding to ESG and regulatory pressure; iFixit partnership extends product lifespan and could reduce churn in Android wearables.
insights.quote: As the first repairable smartwatch from Google, you can swap out the battery and the gorgeous, domed display if necessary, using parts from iFixit.
insights.locator: Best Smartwatch for Android Owners section

insights.claim: Therabody's JetBoots Pro Plus compression boots ($900 after 22% off from $1,150) combine pneumatic compression, vibration, and infrared LED therapies in a wireless form factor—representing consumer-grade professional recovery hardware previously limited to sports medicine settings.
insights.speaker: Boutayna Chokrane
insights.so_what: The $900+ wireless compression boot market signals consumer willingness to pay for professional-grade recovery tech at home; watch Therabody and Hyperice for margin expansion as ASPs in recovery hardware rise.
